title: 201 — Computational Reconstruction of Zhang’s Marxian Formalization
text:
About this course
This course is a sequel to The Calculation Course. It is currently a stub — the syllabus will be written after completing the seminar between Controversies 5 and 6 in the 101 course, once the author has real views about what the sequel should look like.
See the placeholder in the Calculation Course (201/placeholder.qmd) for the full rationale and planned scope.
crumbs: 1  201 — Computational Reconstruction of Zhang's Marxian Formalization

title: 201 — Computational Reconstruction of Zhang’s Marxian Formalization
text:
Prerequisites
Complete all 10 weeks and all 7 controversies of The Calculation Course, with particular attention to:

The seminar on the Chinese critique of the Japanese School (between Controversies 5 and 6)
Controversy 3 (Transformation Problem) and Controversy 5 (Okishio’s Theorem)
The Zhang 2023 paper: “The Formalization of Marx’s Economics” (WRPE 14(1))
crumbs: 1  201 — Computational Reconstruction of Zhang's Marxian Formalization

title: 201 — Computational Reconstruction of Zhang’s Marxian Formalization
text:
Planned scope
Roughly 8–10 weeks covering Zhang’s Vol I formalization program:

Value theory formalization (the mapping f: A → w)
Competition as a dynamic game (prisoner’s dilemma payoff matrices)
Realization uncertainty and crisis probability
The labor process as a cybernetic system
Unemployment dynamics (Zhang’s differential equation)
Capital accumulation and the LTFRP (game-theoretic route)
Primitive accumulation / deprivation process
Unified computational framework

This page will be expanded into a full syllabus when the time comes.
